methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Nordic Air Defence is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Karl Rosander. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector and adjacent-stage comparables, including Series A companies Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), alongside seed-stage peer Tenna Systems ($60M). The broader comp universe includes 10 private defense-tech firms and 2 public peers (DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS).

The peer-median method, the sole applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields a $225M implied valuation. This reflects the median across the four comparables, with a stated range of $60M to $500M. The analysis produces a low-to-high band of $191M to $259M, with $225M as the central estimate. The wide peer range ($60M–$500M) reflects stage dispersion—three comparables occupy Series A while only one sits at seed stage—introducing material uncertainty into any point estimate for a seed company.

The $225M median should be treated as a ceiling estimate pending disclosure of Nordic Air Defence's specific operational metrics, revenue traction, or product differentiation relative to the peer set. The concentration of valuation weight on Series A companies (three of four) may overstate seed-stage value. Refinement would require access to revenue multiples, capital efficiency, or contractual pipeline depth among comparables.
